 {"id":"ft-2xnop","title":"[MEDIUM] pty CommandBuilder serde proptests skip serialized unix umask state","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit c664dc39 (test: extend pty serde proptests) added frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs, but the new roundtrip tests never cover the unix-only `CommandBuilder::umask` field even though it is part of the derived serde surface.

Evidence:
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:202-210 derives Serialize/Deserialize for CommandBuilder and includes `#[cfg(unix)] pub(crate) umask: Option<libc::mode_t>`.
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:423-425 exposes `CommandBuilder::umask(...)` to mutate that serialized field.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:35-52 `build_command(...)` never sets umask.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:57-123 only asserts argv/cwd/env/controlling_tty, so a serde regression that drops or corrupts `umask` would still pass.

Why this matters:
- This commit specifically expanded serde coverage for CommandBuilder, but on Unix it still leaves a serialized execution-affecting field untested.
- `umask` changes file creation permissions for spawned programs, so a silent serde drift here is behaviorally significant.

Requested fix:
- Extend the proptest generator to cover `Option<libc::mode_t>` on unix builds and assert roundtrip preservation.
- If cross-platform friction is a concern, add a targeted unix-only unit/proptest rather than leaving the field uncovered.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:52:51.639844Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.030677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.029943Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-31kaq","title":"proptest(ingest): pin Gap content == current and Content delta.len() < current.len() invariants","description":"proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs pins MR1-MR5 + MR3b (overlap existence) on extract_delta. Two additional shipping contracts are implicit but unchecked: (MR7) every Gap variant returned must carry content == current (the three return sites at ingest.rs:1675, 1713, 1724 all satisfy this, but nothing prevents a future refactor from breaking it); (MR8) when Content(delta) is returned, delta.len() < current.len() strictly — since overlap_len >= 1 for any match in the memchr path. Add both as proptest properties alongside the existing MRs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:20.655704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:02:49.872719Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:02:49.872352Z","close_reason":"Landed in 6ff97e37 (test(proptest/ingest): add MR7 Gap-content-equals-current and MR8 Content-strictly-shorter invariants). Concurrent-agent commit landed the exact same MR7/MR8 additions I wrote; my da87c368 ended up as a beads-tracker-only commit (four pre-existing blockers: ft-uigpj, ft-s2u51, ft-zv3u9 filed).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-326s9","title":"Fix bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls test — config shape contradicts production logic","description":"# Pre-existing mTLS test failure

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 359 verification work. The test
`distributed::tests::bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls` at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:3805` fails reproducibly with:

    client bundle: MissingClientCaPath

## Reproduction
```
cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features distributed \
    --lib bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls -- --nocapture
```

## Root cause
The test constructs a `client_cfg` with `auth_mode =
DistributedAuthMode::Mtls` but without `client_ca_path` populated:

```rust
let mut client_cfg = DistributedConfig::default();
client_cfg.enabled = true;
client_cfg.auth_mode = DistributedAuthMode::Mtls;
client_cfg.tls.enabled = true;
client_cfg.tls.cert_path = Some(client_cert.path().display().to_string());
client_cfg.tls.key_path = Some(client_key.path().display().to_string());
// client_ca_path intentionally left as None.
```

Then it calls `build_tls_bundle(&client_cfg, Some(ca_cert.path()))`. Inside
`build_tls_bundle`, `build_server_config` runs the mTLS branch and requires
`tls.client_ca_path` (the cert used to verify INCOMING client certs). It is
None → `DistributedTlsError::MissingClientCaPath`.

The `server_ca_path` parameter passed to `build_tls_bundle` is used by
`build_client_config` to configure the outgoing connection's trust anchor —
it does NOT populate `tls.client_ca_path`.

## The tension
- `ft-xbnl0.2.4` tick 337 test `build_tls_bundle_rejects_mtls_without_client_ca_path`
  explicitly pins "Mtls auth without client_ca_path → `MissingClientCaPath`"
  as the intended production contract.
- The failing test expects the opposite (that this config shape succeeds).

## Options
1. Fix the test: set `client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path = Some(ca_cert.path().display().to_string())` alongside the existing cert/key.
2. Restructure `build_tls_bundle` to take separate server and client CA paths, or to accept `server_ca_path` as sufficient for mTLS when the client-side bundle is being built.
3. Deprecate the failing test if the config shape was only ever tentative.

## Attribution
- Authored: jemanuel on 2026-04-10 (commit 147b22176).
- Never fixed or touched since.
- Observed failing on 2026-04-18 during ft-xbnl0.2.4 verification sweep.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The bead-scoped check script (`scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh`) uses `--lib tls_`
filter which does not catch this test (the name has `mtls` with no `tls_`
substring). All 73 tests in that filter pass. The failure surfaces only on
broader `--lib distributed::tests::` filter.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.2`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:36.191788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047196Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 362 (commit c799aad4): populated client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path in the test, matching the tick-337 positive-guard contract. Broad distributed::tests:: filter now 154/154 ok (was 153/154).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mtls","pre-existing","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-326s9","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.143481Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-3l5bu","title":"[MEDIUM] RateLimiter.pane_counts HashMap leaks entries for every (pane_id, action) pair that is ever checked","description":"# Defect

## File + line

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs:2103` — `RateLimiter::check`.

## Concrete failure path

Every `check(action, Some(pane_id))` call executes

    let timestamps = self.pane_counts.entry((pane_id, action)).or_default();

which **always** inserts a `Vec<Instant>` under `(pane_id, action)` if it doesn't exist, before any limit logic runs. Same story for `global_counts` keyed by `ActionKind`.

After a pane is destroyed, nothing removes its entries. `prune_old(timestamps, window_start)` removes expired timestamps from the Vec but leaves the Vec itself and the HashMap entry in place. Over the lifetime of a long-running `PolicyEngine`, the HashMap grows monotonically:

* Bound per pane: at most one entry per `ActionKind` variant (finite enum).
* Bound across panes: `unique(pane_id) × |ActionKind|`.

On a 200+-pane swarm churning through ~1000 panes/day over 90 days, that's ~90k × |ActionKind| entries at ~16 bytes key + baseline Vec — not a crash, but a monotonic leak tracked nowhere. **Same class as ft-l6v1r** (`pane_activity_tracker`) and the `BackpressureMetrics::dropped_by_pane` leak I fixed earlier (f2390da9).

## Worse-case path: pane_id reuse

The ft-l6v1r commit observed that wezterm can reuse pane_ids. If a reused pane_id hashes to an old (pane_id, action) entry whose Vec still contains stale timestamps, the new pane inherits the dead pane's rate-limit budget — either under-counted (if the prune pass has already emptied the Vec) or over-counted (if timestamps haven't aged out yet). The current `check` path prunes based on `window_start` which is relative to `Instant::now()`, so stale timestamps from a destroyed pane will age out correctly — but entries without any timestamps will still mis-attribute the **first** rate-limited hit back to the old pane's history in any diagnostic that reads `pane_counts` directly.

## Fix scope

1. `RateLimiter::cleanup_pane(pane_id) -> usize` — remove every `(pane_id, _)` entry from `pane_counts`, return the number removed for telemetry / debug_assert. Cheap: one full iteration.
2. Unit test pinning: entries present before, zero after, unrelated panes untouched, idempotent on panes never inserted.
3. Wiring into the PaneDestroyed teardown path is a separate follow-up bead — `PolicyEngine` is not currently plumbed through `ObservationRuntime::spawn_discovery_task` or `handle_native_event`, and that plumbing is a broader cross-boundary change that belongs on its own bead.

Single-file scope: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:26.564595Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:54:31.244870Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:54:31.244127Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","title":"[asupersync][vendored] Mux client/pool/runtime migration completion","description":"Drive completion of DirectMuxClient/MuxPool/runtime-adjacent migrations and align semantics with core doctrine.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remaining runtime-incompatible primitives in mux client/pool paths are migrated or isolated with explicit adapters.\n2. Cancellation and backpressure semantics match doctrine and are documented.\n3. Unit tests cover mux path behavior under cancellation/retry/load.\n4. Integration tests validate core<->mux interactions after migration.\n5. E2E scripts verify mux reliability and structured diagnostics under stress.","notes":"This task should absorb and harmonize ongoing slices so we converge on one coherent mux runtime model. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:19.410169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:59:26.700552Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mux","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2h5wv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-p48pw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2449,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"SilverBluff starting a bounded 2026-03-22 support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2.  {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","title":"[ft-e34d9.10.5.2][support] Strengthen cancellation-under-contention invariants in mux migration completion harness","description":"Bounded test-only support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2. Result: 1 passed / 0 failed. This confirms the new regression in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs correctly proves a cancelled nested acquire under contention does not leak permits, does not count as a successful checkout, does not issue an extra mux request, and still allows the following healthy release/retry path to restore a balanced pool.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:30:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-e8brh","title":"[MEDIUM] cancel_races_child_creation test has weak race coverage; passes trivially via propagation","description":"In commit 6a16ae0a (fix(ft-2xnop): add unix umask coverage ... +
cancellation concurrency tests), the test
\`cancel_races_child_creation_all_children_see_cancellation\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/src/cancellation.rs:2635-2676 claims to verify
the cancel+child race but does not reliably exercise the race window.

Structure:
\`\`\`rust
for _ in 0..20 {
    let parent = CancellationToken::new(...);
    let n_creators = 8;
    let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(n_creators + 1));

    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..n_creators).map(|i| {
        let parent = parent.clone();
        let barrier = Arc::clone(&barrier);
        thread::spawn(move || {
            barrier.wait();
            parent.child(ScopeId(format!(\"c_{i}\")))
        })
    }).collect();

    barrier.wait();
    parent.cancel(ShutdownReason::UserRequested);
    // ... assert every child is cancelled
}
\`\`\`

Problems:

1. The barrier synchronizes the START of each thread's work but not
   the duration of \`parent.child(...)\`. On a machine with >=8 cores,
   the 8 creator threads typically finish \`parent.child(...)\` BEFORE
   the main thread reaches \`parent.cancel(...)\`. In that ordering, all
   children are pre-existing when cancel propagates — a trivial case
   covered by other tests in the file.

2. 20 sequential rounds with no scheduler perturbation (no \`yield_now\`,
   no sleep, no thread count above core count) rarely hit the interesting
   interleaving where cancel runs mid-child-creation.

3. The assertion passes even if \`parent.child()\` has a bug where it
   reads the \"parent cancelled\" flag AFTER registration but BEFORE
   propagation reaches it — because the test never creates that window.

Impact: false confidence in race-safe \`CancellationToken::child()\`
under concurrent cancel. A latent bug where a newly-created child
escapes both propagation (not-yet-registered) AND post-cancel detection
(child() reads stale flag) would not be caught.

Fix direction:
- Spawn more creators than CPU cores (e.g., 64) so some are preempted
  mid-call.
- Insert \`thread::yield_now()\` or a short sleep inside the creator
  body before calling \`parent.child()\` to widen the race window.
- Alternatively, use an inner barrier that creators wait on AFTER
  they've decided to create the child but BEFORE registration, so the
  main thread can force the interleaving.
- Consider using loom or shuttle for deterministic interleaving
  coverage instead of probabilistic races.

Severity: medium — test quality issue, not a proven correctness bug
in cancellation.rs. Filing because the test claims coverage it does
not deliver.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:11.821489Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626483Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626237Z","close_reason":"Hardened in 7ac677a6: creator count 8→64 (forces preemption on any host), per-thread yield_now + modulo 50µs sleep widens race, rounds 20→64, each creator samples is_cancelled() pre-child() and the round-level outcomes are checked so the test asserts the cancel-first ordering was actually exercised at least once — if it isn't, the test fails with a diagnostic pointing at loom/shuttle for deterministic coverage. Build clean; local run blocked by unrelated macOS dyld bottleneck under load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","concurrency","review","test-gap","testing"]}

 {"id":"ft-eqtcq","title":"aegis_entropy_anomaly::EProcess::update propagates NaN e_value; permanently disables rejection","description":"EProcess::update calls likelihood_ratio(h, baseline_mean, baseline_std, collapse_mean, collapse_std). If any f64 input is NaN, log_h0 and log_h1 both become NaN, log_lr = NaN - NaN = NaN, log_lr.exp() = NaN, and clamp(1e-6, 1e6) passes NaN through because f64::clamp does not reject NaN. self.e_value *= NaN = NaN. The final clamp(1e-100, max_e) at update:226 also passes NaN through. Future is_rejected() returns (NaN >= threshold) = false, silently disabling anomaly detection for the lifetime of this EProcess. Matches ft-b4l62 / ft-yskcu / ft-c17iw / ft-n4fdx pattern — fail-closed on non-finite inputs at the update() boundary.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:03:02.334443Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:05:25.492018Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:05:25.491748Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-yskcu","title":"continuous_backpressure EwmaSmoother + sigmoid NaN poison cascade","description":"## Defect

Same NaN-poison pattern as ft-761tz (disk_pressure), in a sibling subsystem:
\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/continuous_backpressure.rs\`.

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller invokes \`ContinuousBackpressure::update(capture_ratio, write_ratio)\` where
   one ratio is \`NaN\`. Easy trigger: an external smoothed source that hit a div-by-zero
   guard upstream and quietly emitted NaN; or any test/consumer using \`observe_ratio\`
   directly.
2. Line 225-226: \`capture_ratio.clamp(0.0, 1.0)\` returns NaN — \`f64::clamp\` propagates
   NaN by design.
3. Line 228: \`self.capture_smoother.update(NaN)\`. \`EwmaSmoother::update\` (line 105)
   has **no NaN guard** — \`alpha.mul_add(NaN, ...)\` → NaN → \`self.value = NaN\`
   permanently. Every subsequent \`update()\` call preserves the NaN.
4. Line 231: \`smoothed_capture.max(smoothed_write)\` — \`f64::max\` does NOT propagate NaN
   under IEEE-754 (returns the non-NaN operand), but if both are NaN you get NaN.
5. Line 232-236: \`severity(NaN, _, _)\` → calls local \`sigmoid\` (line 140) which does
   \`x.clamp(-500.0, 500.0)\` — same NaN-propagation footgun. Returns NaN.
6. Line 232: \`self.current_severity = NaN\` permanently.
7. \`compute_actions\` (line 287-298) multiplies/mul_adds severity into
   \`poll_multiplier\`, \`pane_skip_fraction\`, \`detection_skip_fraction\`,
   \`buffer_limit_fraction\` — every field becomes NaN. ThrottleActions emitted to
   downstream throttle consumers are entirely poisoned.
8. \`BackpressureSnapshot\` (line 320) exposes \`severity\` and \`queue_ratio\` as
   \`f64\`. JSON serialization of NaN is invalid in strict JSON; \`serde_json\` emits
   \`null\` silently, corrupting telemetry payloads downstream.
9. Lucky accident: \`severity_to_tier(NaN)\` returns Black (NaN < x is always false,
   final else branch), so the discrete tier surface fails closed by IEEE-754
   coincidence rather than design. The continuous severity surface fails OPEN.

## Severity rationale

- Same class of silent wrong answer as ft-761tz.
- Continuous surface (severity, queue_ratio, ThrottleActions) fails OPEN — values
  become NaN, poll_multiplier becomes NaN, downstream throttle math becomes useless.
- Discrete \`equivalent_tier\` happens to fail closed by accident, not by design.
- Telemetry consumers (snapshot serialization) silently lose data.

## Fix sketch

1. \`EwmaSmoother::update\` (line 105) — if \`raw.is_nan()\`, return \`self.value\`
   without state change. Same defense as disk_pressure \`EwmaEstimator::update\`.
2. Local \`sigmoid\` (line 140) — short-circuit \`is_nan()\` to \`0.5\` like the
   backpressure_severity sibling does.
3. \`ContinuousBackpressure::update\` (line 224) — defense in depth: if either
   ratio is NaN, return \`current_actions()\` without state change.
4. Regression tests: pin each guard plus end-to-end NaN→Black-tier→clean recovery.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/continuous_backpressure.rs\` only. No cross-crate API
changes. No changes to \`backpressure.rs\` or \`backpressure_severity.rs\`
(both already hardened).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:05:30.189872Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:19:28.985819Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:19:28.985530Z","close_reason":"Landed in edab97f4 (fix(continuous_backpressure): NaN fail-closed across EmaSmoother, sigmoid, and update)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-yv02l","title":"[P2] Audit placeholder/stub markers surfaced by mock-code-finder sweep","description":"Surfaced by /mock-code-finder static scan of
crates/frankenterm-core/src/ and frankenterm/ (pane 5, session
2026-04-20). Seven findings; none reach ship-blocking severity, but
each warrants a targeted review to either remove the placeholder,
document the invariant that makes it safe, or replace with a real
implementation.

Categories:
  1. TODO/FIXME/XXX in production
  2. unimplemented!() stubs
  3. placeholder values overwritten later
  4. magic fallback values masking platform/error conditions

Findings:

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs:242 (cat 4)
  `collect_system_memory` returns `(0, 0)` on non-Linux/non-macOS.
  Why it matters: a future Windows/BSD target would silently receive
  zero memory readings instead of an error. Consider returning
  `Result<(u64, u64), _>` or documenting the platform contract.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs:1473 (cat 1)
  `// TODO(asupersync): replace with asupersync::select! or ...`
  Why it matters: the only surviving prod TODO in frankenterm-core;
  pane 6 owns the asupersync migration, already tracked by
  ft-xbnl0.2.x epic.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/user_preferences.rs:729 (cat 3)
  `action: UserAction::Ignore, // placeholder` inside the synthetic
  trajectory generator. Why it matters: value is overwritten a few
  lines below via `best_action`, but the placeholder is still
  exposed via `obs_base.action` during reward computation between
  lines 730-755. Review whether any feature extractor reads the
  placeholder value.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/vector_index.rs:126 (cat 3)
  `// placeholder for count — will be patched on finish`. Why it
  matters: if the finish path ever takes an early-return before
  patching, the placeholder ships. Add an assertion at finish.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/dataflow.rs:980 (cat 3)
  `kind: NodeKind::Source, // placeholder`. Why it matters: same
  pattern as vector_index — review the constructor exit paths.

- frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs:4078,4082,4090,4098,4106,4110,4114,
  4141,4144,4150,4158,4161,4164,4170 (cat 2) — 14 `unimplemented!()`
  calls. Why it matters: the cluster needs domain-owner review;
  pane 1 (mux) territory.

- frankenterm/mux/src/pane.rs:609,612,616,624,670,674 (cat 2) — 6
  `unimplemented!()` calls. Why it matters: same cluster as tab.rs,
  pane 1 territory.

No defect shipped from this sweep because the findings are either
low-severity (telemetry fallback on unsupported platforms), tracked
elsewhere (runtime_compat TODO → ft-xbnl0.2.x), or owned by other
panes (mux unimplemented stubs).

Related: in the prior ticks this session I shipped two defensive
fixes for real panic-on-input defects surfaced by similar scans —
0e0668cc (termwiz ReadBuffer out-of-range) and the truncate_for_log
emoji-boundary fix in wait_execution.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:49:35.573939Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:12:56.247438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:12:56.247138Z","close_reason":"Cat-A vector_index fix shipped in 2d522ba7 (parallel agent, functionally identical to my in-progress patch). Cat-B findings validated as benign with documented rationale. Cat-2 mux clusters out-of-scope (pane-1 territory). Full triage table in description.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","code-quality","mock-finder","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-ywlpu","title":"orphan_reaper misparses wezterm cli flag-value forms before subcommand","description":"parse_ps_line_if_reapable() finds the first non-flag token after `cli` and treats it as the subcommand.
That rejects valid invocations like `wezterm cli --config-file /tmp/x.toml list`, where `/tmp/x.toml` is a flag value, not the subcommand.
Result: legitimate short-lived helper processes can become unreapable if FrankenTerm or operators add value-taking CLI flags before the real subcommand.
Tightest fix: teach the parser to skip known value-taking flags (or parse argv shape more robustly) before matching against REAPABLE_SUBCOMMANDS.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:59:49.563841Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:23:51.880516Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:23:51.880272Z","close_reason":"Parser now accepts wezterm cli value-taking flags before reapable subcommands; compiled allowlist check fixed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-z01cl","title":"[reality-gap] ft robot checkpoint/context/work/fleet/profile families return robot.not_implemented","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:37-38 markets "**Robot Mode API**: JSON/TOON interface
optimized for AI agents to control other AI agents" as a core value
prop — "the closest analogy is Kubernetes for terminal-based AI
agents". This is not a footnote; it's in the opening paragraph.

# Reality (code)

Five of the `ft robot` subcommand families are CLI-parsed but
backend-unimplemented. At `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`:

    :22632   RobotCommands::Checkpoint { command } => {
    :22691   RobotCommands::Context    { command } => {
    :22738   RobotCommands::Work       { command } => {
    :22816   RobotCommands::Fleet      { command } => {
    :22876   RobotCommands::Profile    { command } => {

Each arm builds a `RobotNtmCommand`, then calls
`build_ntm_not_implemented_response(&ntm_cmd, elapsed_ms(start))`
(main.rs:10121) which emits a `robot.not_implemented` envelope with:

    "Command `ft robot <family> <action>` is recognized but not yet
     implemented. This command will be available in a future release.
     NTM equivalent: <command>."

So the CLI surface is real, the arg parsing is real, the response
envelope is real — the backend is missing. The "NTM equivalent"
pointer is the current workaround (operator shells out to `ntm`).

# Scope

Subcommands covered by this gap (15+ concrete actions):

- Checkpoint: save, rollback, list
- Context: status, rotate, history
- Work: claim, complete, status, list
- Fleet: status, launch, stop, describe
- Profile: show, list, set

Working `ft robot` surfaces (NOT covered by this gap): state,
trigger, approve — those DO have real backends.

# Gap severity

- README treats Robot Mode as a pillar feature.
- Users following the opening paragraph to `ft robot fleet launch ...`
  get a `robot.not_implemented` envelope at first invocation.
- JSON envelope shape IS honest (structured error code, clear
  message, NTM pointer) — the UX is "recognized but unshipped,"
  not "silently wrong."
- The honest error makes this a DOCS gap more than a correctness
  bug. Priority is for a README disclosure, not an urgent code fix.

# Recommended disposition

Either:
  (a) Add a clear "Currently implemented" subtable under the Robot
      Mode API heading, listing which families ship real backends
      vs. return `robot.not_implemented` (documentation-first,
      1-line-README change, ships today); OR
  (b) Split this into 5 implementation beads (one per family) and
      track them to close. `br` already contains `wa-rsaf` epic and
      the ft-xbnl0 goal-line epic — family-level subbeads would
      hang under one of those.

(a) is the immediate unblocker; (b) is the real closure path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:10.888900Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:46:37.163342Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:46:37.162999Z","close_reason":"Landed docs fix (disposition (a)) in 0e1432fc. Added 'Implementation status' subsection under Robot Mode API heading listing all 13 families — 8 shipped, 5 pending with use-ntm pointer. Verified each pending family (checkpoint/context/work/fleet/profile) against main.rs:22632-22879 build_ntm_not_implemented_response routing. The 5 pending implementations themselves remain under wa-rsaf.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mor","reality-gap","robot"]}
 {"id":"ft-z12h0","title":"Fix frankenterm-core --lib compile blockers in vendored mux client and web_framework","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:35:22.537549Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:11:58.459867Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:11:58.459572Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-z1yhs","title":"session_restore::load_checkpoint_by_id silently accepts mux_pane_state row count ≠ stored pane_count","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:473-481` returns `CheckpointData { pane_count, pane_states }` where:

* `pane_count` is the value stored in `session_checkpoints.pane_count` at save time (stored claim).
* `pane_states` is the rows actually loaded from `mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id = ?1` (actual rows).

Nothing in the load path asserts these match. Line 1204-1205 logs both counts but does not fail on disagreement. Post-ft-ybtyg, the state_hash at `session_checkpoints.state_hash` covers `(session_id, pane_id_map, checkpoint_at)` — NOT the contents or row count of `mux_pane_state`. So:

* Adding a bogus `mux_pane_state` row (checkpoint_id matches) → `pane_states.len() > pane_count`, hash recomputes correctly (unchanged inputs), load accepts the extra row silently.
* Removing a legitimate `mux_pane_state` row → `pane_states.len() < pane_count`, same as above.

A subsequent restore step reconstructs panes from `pane_states`, so an extra row creates a pane that was never in the session, and a missing row silently drops a pane.

# Threat model

DB-write is already fatal, so this is hardening against:
1. Naive filesystem-level tampering (e.g., hostile repo ships a crafted `ft.db` for operator to open).
2. Accidental corruption (storage layer bug drops rows or writes orphan rows, and restore produces non-sensical state with no loud failure).
3. Upgrade drift (checkpoint_id collision between pre-/post-migration rows causes stale rows to get bundled into a new checkpoint's load).

# Fix

In `load_checkpoint_by_id`, after loading `pane_states`, assert `pane_states.len() == pane_count` and return `RestoreError::CorruptCheckpoint` with a descriptive message otherwise. Cost: one integer comparison per load.

The fix does NOT close the more comprehensive gap (hash doesn't cover mux_pane_state CONTENT, only count/identity of rows), but it's a high-signal cheap check that catches naive tampering and accidental drift.

# Regression tests

Two symmetric tests:

* `load_checkpoint_rejects_added_pane_state_row`: create a legit checkpoint, then INSERT an extra `mux_pane_state` row with the same checkpoint_id, call `load_checkpoint_by_id`, assert `CorruptCheckpoint`.

* `load_checkpoint_rejects_deleted_pane_state_row`: same, but DELETE one of the rows, assert `CorruptCheckpoint`.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no crash, no data loss on the happy path. Silent wrong-answer on the tampered/corrupt path. Cheap to fix; closes the detection window between state_hash (which covers the pane_id_map) and the separate mux_pane_state table (which the hash does not cover).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:38.027641Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:06.693662Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:06.693343Z","close_reason":"Withdrawn: the count-mismatch assertion would false-positive in the real write flow where finalize_restore writes session_checkpoints.pane_count BEFORE a separate capture step writes the mux_pane_state rows. load_checkpoint_by_id is a read path that can legitimately see pane_states.len()=0 while pane_count>0 in that window. Re-examine at a higher layer (is the hash scope wrong? is there a two-phase atomicity guarantee?) rather than the naive row-count check I drafted.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
